extensionDescription.message: A provider add-on for WebExtensions using the VFS toolkit, that exposes the local home folder via native messaging.

setupQuestion.message: Grant add-on "$ADDON_NAME$" access to your local home folder?

setupAlreadyGranted.message: Access for add-on "$ADDON_NAME$" has already been granted.

setupReadonlyDesc.message: The add-on can browse and read files in your home folder, but cannot create, modify, or delete anything.

setupReadwriteDesc.message: The add-on can read, create, modify, and delete files and folders in your home folder.

configShowHiddenDesc.message: Include files and folders whose name starts with a dot (e.g. .bashrc).

optionsPageTitle.message: Local Home Folder Access Provider — Options

bannerOkDetail.message: The local file system helper is installed and responding correctly.

bannerErrorDetail.message: The helper application is not installed or not responding. Follow the setup instructions below to get started.

optionsIntroP1.message: This add-on provides filesystem access to your local home folder to any WebExtension using the $TOOLKIT$. After granting access, these WebExtensions can read/write files in your home folder. Because modern WebExtensions cannot directly access the local file system, it uses Mozilla's $NATIVEMSG$ to communicate with a small helper application running on your computer.

optionsArchitectureLi1.message: This add-on receives file system requests from consumer extensions and forwards them to the native helper over a secure local pipe.

optionsArchitectureLi2.message: The native helper runs on your computer and performs the actual file system operations, rooted at your home directory.

optionsIntroP2.message: To get started, download the helper package, extract it, and run the included install script. This registers the helper with Thunderbird so the add-on can communicate with it.

optionsSecurityNotice.message: Security notice: Granting file system access has security implications. Consider whether you really need direct file system access, or if a less intrusive storage backend might work as well.

optionsInstructionsP.message: After downloading, extract the package, open a terminal in the extracted folder, and run the script for your platform:

optionsInstructionsRestart.message: Restart Thunderbird after installation. Python 3 must be installed on your system.

optionsEmptyState.message: No extensions have been granted access yet.

optionsNotice.message: Revoking access removes the stored connection immediately. The extension will lose access to your home folder on its next request.
